patterns.title: SQL Expression INTO OUTFILE allowlist bypass
patterns.description: SQL parser allowlist permits AST node types that cover file-write variants (INTO OUTFILE/DUMPFILE) without field-level inspection. Combined with missing engine-level file-write disable and empty secure_file_priv default.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: 'case *sqlparser.Into:' in allowedNode function without field inspection

confirmed_instances.note: 4 control failures: allowlist, IsReadOnly delegation, DisableFileWrites not set, secure_file_priv empty
INTO DUMPFILE variant -- same *sqlparser.Into allowlist entry, same db.go context, binary raw write via rel.go:659-673; confirmed by TestAllowQueryPermitsIntoDumpfile in repro_test.go
POST /api/v1/eval -- same expr.Service.BuildPipeline execution, only ActionAlertingRuleRead (Viewer) required, direct SQL expression evaluation
K8s aggregated query API -- K8s authorizer only checks user identity; same expr.Service wired; requires queryService or grafanaAPIServerWithExperimentalAPIs flag
Zero negative security test cases in allowlist test suite; no test verifies INTO OUTFILE/DUMPFILE is blocked; enables silent regression of any fix
POST /api/v1/rule/test/grafana (Viewer-accessible preview) and persistent alert rule scheduler (Editor) -- both route through same SQL engine; scheduler creates persistent repeated file writes

patterns.root_cause: AST node type allowlist does not distinguish between safe variants (INTO @var) and dangerous variants (INTO OUTFILE/DUMPFILE). Combined with Into.IsReadOnly() delegating to child, missing WithDisableFileWrites(true), and empty secure_file_priv default.

patterns.title: Default secret JWT forgery to full API auth bypass
patterns.description: JWT authentication using a default secret value that is trivially known. Combined with authn client that fires on all endpoints without scoping, enabling forged JWT to authenticate as Admin on any API.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: jwt.ParseWithClaims($KEY, $CLAIMS, func($T) { return []byte($CFG_VALUE) }) where $CFG_VALUE has a hardcoded default

confirmed_instances.note: Reconfirmed in chamber-3 fresh audit: default renderer_token '-' used as HMAC-SHA512 key
renderKey cookie Test() fires on ALL endpoints with no path/endpoint scoping; render keys are valid for admin API endpoints for their full TTL; no feature flag required
getRenderUserFromCache trusts gob-decoded RenderUser from remote cache without integrity check; attacker who writes to Redis/Memcached can authenticate as Admin without renderAuthJWT flag
hashOAuthState uses cfg.SecretKey (default SW2YcwTIb9zpOOhoPsMm) as critical factor; forged OAuth state enables CSRF account takeover when clientSecret is empty or known
jwtRenderKeyProvider.buildJWTClaims sets only ExpiresAt; no iss/aud/jti/nbf claims; any JWT signed with same renderer_token is valid across all instances in a cluster
Advisor checks [security] secret_key but not [rendering] renderer_token; no startup validation that renderer_token != '-' when renderAuthJWT is enabled; silent unsafe default

patterns.root_cause: Single-byte default secret for JWT signing combined with no audience/endpoint scoping on the authn client

variant_hunt.coverage: Exhaustive search across: all authn clients and priority queue ordering; render key cache path (getRenderUserFromCache); OAuth state CSRF (hashOAuthState with cfg.SecretKey); JWT missing claim fields (iss/aud/jti/nbf); advisor security check coverage gaps. M13 (renderer HTTP mode X-Auth-Token default) and M14 (secret_key password reset forgery) are existing confirmed findings covering the same root pattern.

patterns.title: Go middleware construction without invocation (dead RBAC check)
patterns.description: Web middleware handler is constructed via function composition but the resulting handler function is never called with the request context, making the security check dead code.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: ac.Middleware($AC)($EVAL) without subsequent ($C) call in same function body
detection_signature.codeql: Custom query: find middleware handler construction without invocation

confirmed_instances.note: SnapshotPublicModeOrDelete -- RBAC check for ActionSnapshotsDelete never executed
SnapshotPublicModeOrCreate -- RBAC check for ActionSnapshotsCreate never executed

patterns.root_cause: Function composition pattern in Go where the return value (a callable handler) is silently discarded

variant_hunt.coverage: Exhaustive: all 18 ac.Middleware() usages in non-test Go files enumerated; all except auth.go:249 and auth.go:266 use the correct pattern (assign to variable, pass as route middleware). req* middleware variables, pkg/middleware/ files, and api.go route registrations all verified. Pattern is confined to the two instances already documented in p8-001.

patterns.title: reqSignedIn vs reqSignedInNoAnonymous anonymous bypass pattern
patterns.description: Routes using reqSignedIn are accessible to anonymous users when [auth.anonymous] is enabled. The fix is to use reqSignedInNoAnonymous which sets ReqNoAnonynmous=true.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: reqSignedIn used on routes that should not be anonymous-accessible

confirmed_instances.note: /avatar/:hash endpoint
/render/* endpoint -- headless browser DoS when anonymous auth enabled; variant confirmed in phase-10 hunt
/api/gnet/* endpoint -- unauthenticated use of GrafanaComSSOAPIToken as reverse proxy; variant confirmed in phase-10 hunt

patterns.root_cause: reqSignedIn does not enforce ReqNoAnonynmous flag, allowing anonymous access bypass

patterns.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify in plugin UI
patterns.description: React component renders external HTML content via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify sanitization, enabling stored XSS via supply chain (plugin catalog).

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: $VAR }} where $VAR is not wrapped in DOMPurify.sanitize()

confirmed_instances.note: plugin.details.readme -- reconfirmed in chamber-3 fresh audit
plugin.details.changelog -- same pattern, remote GCOM data unsanitized
plugin.details.statusContext -- deprecated plugin catalog data via renderMarkdown (xss lib, not DOMPurify); variant confirmed in phase-10 hunt
query_help markdown from plugin files via /api/plugins/:id/markdown/query_help; affects all data source users not just admins; variant confirmed in phase-10 hunt

patterns.root_cause: Inconsistent application of DOMPurify sanitization across plugin UI components

patterns.title: K8s legacy store missing org filter on key-based lookup
patterns.description: K8s API legacy store implementations perform database lookups by key/deleteKey without including org_id in the WHERE clause, enabling cross-org operations. The CVE fix applied to the legacy REST API was not ported to the K8s API path.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: GetDashboardSnapshot(ctx, &GetDashboardSnapshotQuery{Key: $VAR}) without subsequent OrgID check

confirmed_instances.note: Delete() -- no org filter
Get() -- no org filter
DeleteWithKey via custom route -- no org filter
deleteKeyREST.Connect() -- calls SnapshotLegacyStore.Get() via unwrapped snapshotDualWrite; no org filter; returns plaintext deleteKey of cross-org snapshot
dashboardREST.Connect() -- calls SnapshotLegacyStore.Get() via unwrapped snapshotDualWrite; no org filter; returns full dashboard JSON payload of cross-org snapshot

patterns.root_cause: CVE fix applied to REST API path but not ported to K8s API path; K8s namespace scoping assumed to provide org isolation but store layer does not enforce it

patterns.title: Service interface missing orgID parameter for cross-org isolation
patterns.description: Service method signature omits orgID parameter, causing SQL operations to execute without org_id constraint. Other methods on the same interface correctly include orgID.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: func ($S *Service) $METHOD(ctx context.Context, $UID string, $UID2 string) error where sibling methods include orgID int64

confirmed_instances.note: CancelSnapshot missing orgID

patterns.root_cause: Inconsistent interface design -- some methods include orgID, others do not

variant_hunt.coverage: All 8 Cloud Migration service interface methods reviewed: GetToken, CreateToken, ValidateToken, DeleteToken, CreateSession, GetSession, GetSessionList, DeleteSession, CreateSnapshot, GetSnapshot, GetSnapshotList, UploadSnapshot. All methods except CancelSnapshot correctly pass orgID to the store layer.

patterns.title: Empty allowlist defaults to allow-all (inverted security assumption)
patterns.description: IP/host allowlist implementation returns true when the list is empty, creating a dangerous default where no configuration means no restriction. Should default to deny-all when empty.

detection_signature.codeql: Custom query: find allowlist checks that return true on empty list

confirmed_instances.note: Auth proxy isAllowedIP returns true when acceptedIPs is empty
checkWhiteList() returns true when DataProxyWhiteList is empty (default); SSRF protection is OFF by default
isEmailAllowed() returns true when allowedDomains is empty (default for all OAuth providers); any OAuth-authenticated user can log in
isGroupMember() returns true when AllowedGroups is empty; used by Google/GitLab/AzureAD/Okta/generic OAuth
isTeamMember() returns true when teamIds is empty; GitHub team restriction is no-op by default
isOrganizationMember() returns true when allowedOrganizations is empty; GitHub org restriction is no-op by default
isTeamMember() returns true when teamIds is empty; generic OAuth team restriction is no-op by default
isOrganizationMember() returns true when allowedOrganizations is empty; generic OAuth org restriction is no-op by default
isOrganizationMember() returns true when allowedOrganizations is empty; Grafana.com OAuth org restriction is no-op by default

patterns.root_cause: Empty allowlist treated as allow-all rather than deny-all

patterns.title: Attacker-controlled encryption key via untrusted server response
patterns.description: Application receives an encryption public key from an external server response and uses it to encrypt sensitive data without validating the key's provenance, format, or binding to a trusted party. When combined with SSRF that redirects the server connection to an attacker, the attacker supplies their own key and can decrypt all exfiltrated data.

detection_signature.semgrep: pattern: json.Decode($RESP).($STRUCT) where $STRUCT contains a []byte field used in crypto operations
detection_signature.codeql: Custom query: find deserialized public keys used in encryption without validation

confirmed_instances.note: StartSnapshotResponse.GMSPublicKey used in NaCl encryption without validation

untested_candidates: Any other external API responses that provide encryption keys consumed by Grafana
patterns.root_cause: Trusting encryption key material from an external server response without key pinning, certificate chain validation, or provenance verification

patterns.title: Empty Origin bypass in WebSocket origin check
patterns.description: WebSocket origin validation function unconditionally accepts connections when the Origin header is empty/absent, bypassing all configured origin allowlists. This creates a Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking vector in deployments behind reverse proxies that strip Origin.

confirmed_instances.note: getCheckOriginFunc returns true for empty Origin
checkSameHost returns nil for empty Origin

patterns.root_cause: RFC 6455 Section 10.2 recommends checking Origin; the code interprets absent Origin as 'same-origin' rather than 'unknown/reject'

variant_hunt.coverage: All 9 Go files importing gorilla/websocket or using websocket in pkg/ reviewed. No browser-facing WebSocket upgraders beyond the two confirmed instances exist. Loki streaming.go uses websocket as an outbound client (dial), not a server upgrade. No plugin packages import gorilla/websocket.
